introSection: Wild Hog was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Magic Springs & Crystal Falls in Hot Springs, Arkansas, USA from 1978 to 1984.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Some of the ride's cars were reportedly being re-engineered in 1979 to traverse the track backward.

On December 11, 1981 Magic Springs reached an $87,500 settlement out-of-court with a father, who filed the suit following an incident on June 19. The man alleged that the ride stopped suddenly causing his children to be thrown forward. His 13-year-old daughter lost ten teeth after hitting her face and his 4-year-old son suffered cuts and abrasions. The children's grandmother was also on the ride and suffered a broken arm.

fullText:
Wild Hog was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Magic Springs & Crystal Falls in Hot Springs, Arkansas, USA from 1978 to 1984.

Some of the ride's cars were reportedly being re-engineered in 1979 to traverse the track backward.

On December 11, 1981 Magic Springs reached an $87,500 settlement out-of-court with a father, who filed the suit following an incident on June 19. The man alleged that the ride stopped suddenly causing his children to be thrown forward. His 13-year-old daughter lost ten teeth after hitting her face and his 4-year-old son suffered cuts and abrasions. The children's grandmother was also on the ride and suffered a broken arm.
